title: pyrus albensis
intro: Pyrus albensis, also known as the White Pear or Snow Pear, is a species of pear tree native to the mountainous regions of China. The bark and leaves of Pyrus albensis have been used in traditional Chinese medicine for their medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bark contains flavonoids and glycosides, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, providing health benefits such as reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. Herbal preparations of Pyrus albensis include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to calm the respiratory system and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
